request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

prior_feedback.note: Switch supplier from generic to Sigma-Aldrich; trehalose grade should be molecular biology grade, NOT pharmaceutical.
HeLa post-thaw recovery needs at least 48 hours, never 24 hours.

hypothesis.intervention: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: trehalose sucrose cryoprotectant HeLa post-thaw viability
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

references.title: Effect of Trehalose and Sucrose in Post-thaw Quality of Crassostrea angulata Sperm
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses the effects of trehalose and sucrose on post-thaw quality, which is relevant to the proposed hypothesis about cryoprotectants, although it focuses on sperm rather than HeLa cells.

references.title: Impacts of adding sucrose or trehalose to extenders with different glycerol concentrations on freezablility and fertility of buffalo bull semen
references.relevance_note: This study investigates the use of trehalose and sucrose in cryopreservation protocols, which is directly related to the intervention of the hypothesis, but again focuses on buffalo semen rather than HeLa cells.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention of replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant is similar to the work described in papers [2] and [5], which explore the effects of trehalose and sucrose in cryopreservation contexts. However, these studies focus on sperm rather than HeLa cells, indicating a related but different biological system. The experiment type of comparative cryopreservation is also present in these papers, but the specific biological system (HeLa cells) is not directly addressed in the retrieved literature.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with trehalose versus DMSO as a cryoprotectant.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be divided into two groups: one group will be cryopreserved using trehalose and the other using DMSO. Each treatment will be replicated three times to ensure statistical validity. Post-thaw viability will be assessed using trypan blue exclusion after a 30-minute recovery period at 37°C.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved with 10% DMSO in freezing medium.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Cell Culture Preparation
Culture HeLa cells in DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin at 37°C, 5% CO2 until reaching 80% confluency (approximately 48 h).
Equipment: Thermo Scientific Heracell 150i incubator, T25 tissue culture flasks
Reagents: DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092), Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS, Sigma, F7524), Penicillin-Streptomycin (Gibco, 15140-122)
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 80% confluency.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Cryoprotectant Preparation
Prepare two freezing media: (1) 10% DMSO in DMEM and (2) 10% trehalose in DMEM. Filter-sterilize both solutions using a 0.22 µm filter.
Equipment: Sterile 50 mL conical tubes, 0.22 µm filter unit
Reagents: DMSO (Sigma, D8418), Trehalose dihydrate (Sigma, T9531), DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092)
Expected outcome: Both freezing media are sterile and ready for use.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA solution, neutralize with complete DMEM, and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature. Discard supernatant and resuspend cells in freezing media.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, Eppendorf 5 mL pipette tips
Reagents: 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056)
Expected outcome: Cells are successfully detached and resuspended in freezing media.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Cryopreservation
Aliquot 1 mL of cell suspension (1 x 10^6 cells/mL) into cryovials. Freeze cells using a controlled-rate freezer (e.g., NBS Cryo 1°C/min) to -80°C for 24 h, then transfer to liquid nitrogen for long-term storage.
Equipment: NBS Cryo controlled-rate freezer
Expected outcome: Cells are frozen to -80°C and stored in liquid nitrogen.

DAY 2 | 5 min | Thawing Cells
Retrieve cryovials from liquid nitrogen and immediately thaw in a 37°C water bath until just thawed (approximately 2-3 min).
Equipment: 37°C water bath
Expected outcome: Cells are thawed without damage.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Post-thaw Recovery
Transfer thawed cells to 10 mL of complete DMEM and incubate at 37°C for 30 min to allow recovery.
Equipment: Thermo Scientific Heracell 150i incubator
Expected outcome: Cells recover in complete DMEM.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Viability Assessment
After recovery, perform trypan blue exclusion assay. Mix 10 µL of cell suspension with 10 µL of 0.4% trypan blue, load onto a hemocytometer, and count viable (unstained) and non-viable (stained) cells under a microscope.
Equipment: Countess II FL Automated Cell Counter, hemocytometer
Reagents: 0.4% Trypan Blue (Gibco, 15250-061)
Expected outcome: Viability percentage calculated from cell counts.

DAY 2 | 15 min | Data Collection
Record the percentage of viable cells for both trehalose and DMSO treatments. Ensure to replicate this step three times for statistical analysis.
Expected outcome: Data collected for statistical analysis.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Statistical Analysis
Conduct statistical analysis using a two-tailed t-test to compare post-thaw viability between trehalose and DMSO groups. Set significance level at p < 0.05.
Equipment: GraphPad Prism 9
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined between groups.

DAY 2 | 2 days | Replication
Repeat the entire experiment (steps 1-10) two additional times to ensure reproducibility of results.
Expected outcome: Results are consistent across all three replicates.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Trehalose provides superior cryoprotection compared to DMSO for HeLa cells.
Post-thaw recovery time is sufficient for cell viability assessment.
The trypan blue exclusion method accurately reflects cell viability.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability with trehalose is at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same lot of HeLa cells for all experiments.
Maintain consistent freezing and thawing rates across trials.
Calibrate the cell counter before each experiment.
feedback_incorporated:

1. replicate gate in plan text
2. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting
3. change one

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
